pi_items.source: Web search for Omaha, Nebraska standard coordinates.
Web search for Omaha, Nebraska standard coordinates.
Web search for Omaha, Nebraska average elevation (1090 ft = 332 m).
Web search for GOES-16 and GOES-18 HRIT downlink frequency, confirming 1694.1 MHz.

Web search for SES-1 satellite Ku-band beacon frequencies. The real beacon frequencies are 11701.0 MHz and 12199.0 MHz. The prompt instructs the agent to use 11.7 GHz, which is why the metadata has 11700000000, but the precise real-world value is 11701000000 Hz.

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on synthetically generated `.sat` files injected via `setup_task.sh`. The setup deliberately maps the NORAD ID 43013 (which belongs to the Zuma satellite) to GOES-16, whose real NORAD ID is 41866, and uses artificial orbital parameters (e.g., exactly 0.0100 inclination). The ground station parameters for Omaha, Nebraska (41.2565N, 95.9345W, 332m altitude) precisely match the real-world standard coordinates and average elevation for the city. The required downlink frequency for GOES-16 and GOES-18 (1694.1 MHz) accurately reflects their real HRIT broadcasts. However, the requested SES-1 Ku-band beacon frequency of 11.7 GHz (11700000000 Hz) simplifies the true operational beacon frequencies of 11701.0 MHz and 12199.0 MHz.
